step_rationale: Vasoconstriction, endothelial dysfunction, LOX-1 upregulation, and pro-inflammatory effects.
mechanism: Angiotensin II drives endothelial activation, impairs vasodilation, and amplifies plaque inflammation via LOX-1 and NADPH-oxidase signaling.

references.title: The clinical significance of endocardial endothelial dysfunction.
Insight into the mode of action of ACE inhibition in coronary artery disease: the ultimate 'EUROPA' story.
The role of inflammatory stress in acute coronary syndrome.
The role of LOX-1, a novel lectin-like receptor for oxidized low density lipoprotein, in atherosclerosis.
